projects.name: Tond Tenga
projects.description: The first project globally to register under an ICVCM-approved VCS methodology. Restoring 12,000 ha of degraded land across four regions of Burkina Faso through native tree planting and agroforestry, in partnership with 185 villages. All carbon revenues flow directly to communities. Part of the Great Green Wall initiative. 40-year project projecting 3.7M tCO2e removal.

projects.name: Mikoko Pamoja
projects.description: The first blue carbon project in the world to fund conservation through carbon credits. A community-led mangrove conservation and restoration initiative in Gazi Bay on the south coast of Kenya, protecting 117 ha of mangrove forest. Revenue funds clean water, school construction, and school books. UN Equator Prize winner 2017. Certified under Plan Vivo, not Verra.

projects.name: Save the Sand
projects.description: A community co-owned afforestation project in the Sabie-Sand River Catchment in Mpumalanga, restoring 40,000 ha of degraded land through native tree planting and agroforestry. Structured as a carbon pre-sale rather than grant-funded, with a four-tier community benefit-sharing structure. An Article 6.2 ITMO pathway is in progress, positioning the project for compliance market access.

projects.name: Pendjari and W-Benin National Parks REDD+ Project
projects.developer: African Parks Benin (A Branch of African Parks Network)
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting the Pendjari and W National Parks in northern Benin, part of the W-Arly-Pendjari transboundary complex - one of the largest contiguous ecosystems in West Africa and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Managed by African Parks under a 25-year concession agreement with the Beninese government. The project protects habitat for lions, elephants, hippopotamuses, and buffalo.

projects.name: Kalahari Grasslands Project
projects.description: Savanna management project in the Kalahari region of Botswana using fire management and improved grazing practices to enhance carbon stocks across semi-arid grasslands. Developed by Gazelle Ecosolutions, the project works with local communities and cattle farming operations to transition from conventional to regenerative land management.

projects.name: Soil Carbon Management For Improved Grassland: Limpopo-Lipadi, Botswana
projects.description: Agricultural land management project improving soil carbon in degraded grasslands on the Limpopo-Lipadi river system in eastern Botswana. The project applies improved grazing management and soil restoration techniques to restore carbon stocks in communal grazing lands. A relatively small pilot project under the VM0032 soil carbon methodology.

projects.name: Burkina Faso Agricultural Carbon Project (Bufacap)
projects.description: Large-scale agricultural land management project in Burkina Faso applying improved farming practices across smallholder plots to build soil organic carbon. Developed by the national REDD+ secretariat, the project integrates agroforestry, soil fertility management, and sustainable land use across multiple agro-ecological zones.

projects.name: Agroforestry Project of Cameroon
projects.description: Small-scale agroforestry and reforestation project in Cameroon combining food crop cultivation with native tree species to restore degraded farmland. The project works with smallholder farmers to establish multi-strata agroforestry systems that sequester carbon while improving food security and soil fertility.

projects.name: Cameroon Mangrove Ecosystem Restoration & Resilience (CAMERR)
projects.description: Mangrove restoration and conservation project along the coast of Cameroon, focusing on degraded mangrove ecosystems in the Gulf of Guinea. Developed by Planete Urgence, the project works with coastal communities to restore mangrove cover, protect shorelines, and sustain artisanal fisheries dependent on healthy mangrove habitat.

projects.name: Forest Conservation in Boumba-et-Ngoko
projects.description: Improved forest management project protecting lowland tropical rainforest in the Boumba-et-Ngoko department of southern Cameroon, adjacent to the Nki and Boumba Bek National Parks. The project reduces logging pressure through REDD+ financing while maintaining selective timber extraction under reduced-impact logging standards.

projects.name: Greenzone Reforestation Project
projects.description: Small-scale reforestation project in Cameroon establishing native forest species on degraded agricultural land. The project operates at village scale, working with community land holders to restore tree cover on fallow and degraded plots through a combination of direct planting and assisted natural regeneration.

projects.name: Increasing Carbon Stocks through the Reforestation of the Communal Forests of Ndélélé and Bétaré-Oya
projects.description: Reforestation project restoring communal forests in the Ndelele and Betare-Oya departments of eastern Cameroon, a region affected by agricultural encroachment and charcoal production. Developed by Terraformation, the project uses native species and community-based management to rebuild forest cover and carbon stocks.

projects.name: Project to Restore Degraded Lands by Planting Forest and Fruit Trees in Northern Cameroon
projects.description: Reforestation project in the arid Sahel zone of northern Cameroon planting a mix of native forest species and fruit trees on severely degraded lands. The project combines carbon sequestration with food security objectives, establishing productive agroforestry systems that provide direct livelihood benefits to participating communities.

projects.name: Boutili Afforestation & Agroforestry Project
projects.description: Afforestation and agroforestry project in the Central African Republic working with smallholder farmers in the Boutili region to establish trees on degraded farmland. Managed by CENTRAFOREST, the project combines food crop production with native tree cultivation to restore soil fertility and sequester carbon in one of the world's most vulnerable conflict-affected nations.

projects.name: Chinko Conservation Area Redd+ Project, Central African Republic
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting the Chinko Nature Reserve in the far east of the Central African Republic, a 17,600 km2 protected area that safeguards one of the last major intact savanna-forest mosaics in Central Africa. Managed by African Parks under a 50-year government agreement, Chinko protects lions, elephants, buffalo, and critically endangered giant eland in a region previously devastated by armed groups and poaching.

projects.name: Pama project, Central African Republic
projects.description: Reforestation project in the Central African Republic establishing native forest and agroforestry systems on degraded lands in the Pama region. Developed by FRM Commitment, the project works with local communities affected by conflict and displacement to restore tree cover and create long-term income from carbon credits.

projects.name: REDD+ Mwali Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting the remaining native forest on Moheli (Mwali), the smallest and least developed island of the Comoros archipelago. Moheli is home to the Moheli Marine Park and harbours rare endemic species including the Livingstone fruit bat. The project works with the island government to halt deforestation driven by subsistence agriculture.

projects.name: Baulu Project, Democratic Republic Of Congo
projects.description: Improved forest management project in the Baulu forest concession in Equateur Province, DRC, transitioning a former logging concession to a protected REDD+ zone. Developed by FRM Commitment, the project halts further industrial logging and works with surrounding communities to manage the forest sustainably for carbon credits, biodiversity, and community livelihoods.

projects.name: DR Congo Mangrove Blue Carbon Project
projects.description: Mangrove conservation and restoration project along the DRC Atlantic coastline in Bas-Congo Province, protecting one of Central Africa's most significant mangrove ecosystems. Developed by ClimeTrek, the project works with coastal fishing communities to halt mangrove clearance for charcoal and agriculture while restoring degraded mangrove zones.

projects.name: DRC REDD+ Carbon Credit Harvesting Project
projects.description: Large-scale REDD+ project protecting tropical rainforest across multiple forest concessions in the DRC Congo Basin, one of the world's most significant carbon stores and biodiversity reservoirs. The project operates across a network of forest areas threatened by agricultural expansion, artisanal logging, and charcoal production.

projects.name: Grand Katanga Reforestation Project
projects.description: Large-scale reforestation project in Katanga Province, DRC, restoring degraded miombo woodland and savanna on land depleted by charcoal production and subsistence farming. Developed by Carbon Ventures Africa, the project establishes native miombo species across degraded land in one of the continent's most mineral-rich but ecologically damaged regions.

projects.name: Isangi REDD+ Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting tropical forest in the Isangi forest concession in Tshopo Province, DRC. The project works within a former logging concession to halt deforestation and forest degradation while providing alternative livelihoods to communities previously dependent on timber extraction. Protected forest includes critical habitat for bonobos and forest elephants.

projects.name: Kokolopori Bonobo Peace Forest Grouped REDD Project
projects.description: Community-based REDD+ project in Equateur Province, DRC, protecting bonobo habitat across the Kokolopori Bonobo Reserve and surrounding community forests. The reserve protects one of the largest remaining wild bonobo populations and was established by the local Bongandu people as a community-managed conservation area. Carbon revenues fund anti-poaching patrols, community health workers, and local schools.

projects.name: Kwango River Project, Democratic Republic of Congo
projects.description: Reforestation project on the Kwango River plateau in Kwilu Province, DRC, establishing native tree species on degraded savanna and farmland in one of Central Africa's most deforested regions. Managed by FRM Commitment, the project works with local communities to restore tree cover and create long-term income through carbon credits.

projects.name: Lisala REDD+ Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting tropical forest in the Lisala territory of Mongala Province, DRC. Developed by Taranis Operations, the project works within a landscape of forest concessions and community forests threatened by slash-and-burn agriculture and artisanal logging, providing an alternative revenue model for forest conservation.

projects.name: Lukolela REDD+ Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting tropical rainforest in Lukolela Territory, Equateur Province, DRC. Developed by Taranis Operations, the project operates in a remote forest landscape along the Congo River threatened by charcoal production and subsistence agriculture, offering conservation payments as an alternative livelihood for local communities.

projects.name: National REDD+ Project in the Democratic Republic of Congo
projects.description: A portfolio-scale REDD+ project spanning multiple forest concessions across the DRC Congo Basin, representing one of the largest carbon projects by area ever registered under VCS. The project seeks to protect vast areas of intact tropical forest across jurisdictions threatened by agricultural encroachment and logging pressure.

projects.name: Nkuba REDD+ Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting forest buffer zones around Kahuzi-Biega National Park in South Kivu Province, DRC, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and the last stronghold of the eastern lowland (Grauer) gorilla - the world's largest gorilla subspecies, now critically endangered. Managed by the Dian Fossey Gorilla Fund, the project works with communities surrounding the park to reduce deforestation pressure and protect gorilla habitat.

projects.name: Permaculture initiative through community participation
projects.description: Community-based reforestation project in DRC applying permaculture and agroforestry principles to restore degraded agricultural land around village communities in Equateur and Mai-Ndombe provinces. Managed by Allcot, the project trains smallholder farmers in permanent agriculture techniques that combine carbon sequestration with improved food production.

projects.name: Sankuru Peace Forest Grouped REDD Project
projects.developer: Fauna & Flora International / local community partners
projects.description: Large-scale community REDD+ project protecting tropical forest across the Sankuru landscape in Kasai-Oriental Province, DRC. The project works with multiple indigenous and local communities to establish a network of community-managed conservation areas in a forest landscape that is critical habitat for the bonobo and forest elephant.

projects.name: The Mai Ndombe REDD+ Project
projects.description: One of the largest and most recognised REDD+ projects in Africa, protecting tropical forest in the Mai Ndombe Province of the DRC Congo Basin. Developed by Wildlife Works Carbon, the project works with 75,000 community members across 26 villages to protect over 750,000 ha of intact rainforest from deforestation. Community benefits include health clinics, schools, water systems, and direct benefit-sharing payments.

projects.name: The Nkuba REDD+ Project
projects.description: Community-based REDD+ and reforestation project in the Kahuzi-Biega buffer zone in eastern DRC, combining forest protection with active tree planting to create a green corridor that connects fragmented forest habitats for eastern lowland gorillas and chimpanzees. Managed by the Dian Fossey Gorilla Fund in partnership with local communities.

projects.name: Tshopo Lomami Grouped REDD Project
projects.description: Grouped REDD+ project protecting forest across multiple concessions in Tshopo and Lomami Provinces of the DRC Congo Basin. The project aggregates community forest management agreements across a fragmented forest landscape to create a financially viable REDD+ programme at scale, reducing deforestation while providing community income.

projects.name: Djibouti Mangrove Restoration Program
projects.description: Mangrove restoration programme along the arid coastline of Djibouti, targeting degraded mangrove stands in the Khor Ambado and Godoria areas. Given Djiboutis extreme aridity and rapid urbanisation, the project represents a rare restoration effort in one of the worlds least forested countries, with mangroves providing critical nursery habitat for the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden fisheries.

projects.name: Conservation and Restoration of Mangroves in the Coastal Blue Carbon Ecosystem of Egypt
projects.description: Blue carbon project protecting and restoring mangrove ecosystems along Egypt's Red Sea and Mediterranean coastlines, where historical mangrove clearance for coastal development and aquaculture has left degraded habitat. The project works with local fishing communities and the Egyptian government to restore mangrove stands and protect remaining intact areas.

projects.name: Go Green Jojoba Afforestation Project
projects.description: Afforestation project establishing jojoba (Simmondsia chinensis) plantations on degraded desert land in Egypt, a drought-tolerant crop that can sequester carbon in arid conditions where conventional forestry is not viable. Managed by the Egyptian Carbon Center, the project rehabilitates degraded land in the Western Desert while providing a commercial non-food crop that does not compete with food production.

projects.name: AfriScout Regenerative Grazing Project
projects.description: Regenerative grazing project in the Ethiopian highlands and lowlands working with pastoralist communities to restore soil carbon through improved herd management and rotational grazing. Developed by AfriScout using mobile technology to track grazing patterns, the project shifts livestock management from continuous overgrazing to planned rotational systems that allow pasture recovery and soil carbon accumulation.

projects.name: Bale Mountains Eco-region REDD+ project
projects.description: Landmark REDD+ project protecting the Afromontane forests and peatlands of the Bale Mountains in southeastern Ethiopia, the largest tropical mountain peatland in Africa. Managed by the Oromia Forest and Wildlife Enterprise, the project protects critical water catchments for millions of people and habitat for the endangered Ethiopian wolf and mountain nyala. Carbon revenues fund community development and ranger salaries across 68 kebeles.

projects.name: East African Afforestation, Reforestation and Revegetation Program
projects.developer: Horn of Africa Regional Environment Center and Network (HoA-REC&N)
projects.description: Reforestation programme run by the Horn of Africa Regional Environment Centre and Network across degraded highland landscapes in Ethiopia, planting native tree species to restore watershed function and soil carbon. The project works through community forestry groups to establish nurseries and planting programmes in degraded hillside areas.

projects.name: Ethiopia Agroforestry Project On Small-Scale Coffee Parcels
projects.description: Agroforestry project integrating shade trees into smallholder coffee gardens in the Kaffa and Bench-Maji zones of southwestern Ethiopia, the global centre of origin of Arabica coffee. Developed by the PURE PROJECT, the programme supports forest coffee production under native shade trees, sequestering carbon while protecting the genetic diversity of wild Arabica and improving coffee quality and farmer income.

projects.name: Grande Sun Gabon Ogooué-Ivindo REDD+ Project
projects.developer: Hainan Grande Sun Carbon Energy Technology Co.,LTD
projects.description: Large-scale REDD+ project protecting intact rainforest in the Ogooue-Ivindo Province of northeastern Gabon, bordering the Ivindo National Park and its UNESCO-listed Lopé-Okanda ecosystem. The project area contains some of Central Africa's last intact old-growth lowland rainforest, supporting forest elephants, western lowland gorillas, and chimpanzees.

projects.name: Leconi Agroforestry Project in Haut-Ogooue (LAPHO)
projects.description: Large-scale agroforestry project in the Haut-Ogooue and other provinces of Gabon, establishing native tree species and food crops on degraded savanna grasslands. Developed by AERA Group, the project works with rural communities to transform unproductive degraded land into productive agroforestry systems that sequester carbon and provide timber, food, and income.

projects.name: Mandji Improvement
projects.description: Improved forest management project in a commercial timber concession on Mandji Island in the Ogooue-Maritime Province of Gabon, upgrading conventional logging operations to reduced-impact logging (RIL) standards. Managed by Compagnie des Bois du Gabon, the project reduces carbon emissions from timber harvesting while maintaining a sustainable commercial logging operation.

projects.name: Mandji Protection
projects.description: Forest protection component of the Mandji concession project in Gabon's Ogooue-Maritime Province, protecting a portion of the concession from any logging under a set-aside conservation zone. Managed by Compagnie des Bois du Gabon alongside the adjacent Mandji Improvement project, this component permanently protects the highest conservation value forest areas within the concession.

projects.name: Conservation And Restoration Of The Mangrove Ecosystem In The Gambia Through The Redd+ Mechanism
projects.description: Mangrove conservation and restoration project in The Gambia protecting and restoring coastal mangrove ecosystems along the Gambia River and its tributaries. The project works with fishing communities who depend on healthy mangroves for fish nursery habitat and shoreline protection, using community conservation agreements to halt mangrove clearance for charcoal and fuelwood.

projects.name: Atebubu and Wiase Forest Landscape Restoration Project
projects.description: Forest landscape restoration project in the Bono East Region of Ghana restoring degraded farmland and forest reserves through native tree planting and assisted natural regeneration. Developed by iNovaLand, the project works with smallholder farmers in the transition zone between the forest and savanna to restore tree cover in a landscape fragmented by decades of cocoa farming expansion.

projects.name: Bandai Hills Bamboo Reforestation Project, Ghana
projects.description: Bamboo reforestation project in the Bandai Hills area of Ghana's Brong-Ahafo Region establishing commercial bamboo plantations on degraded and marginal agricultural land. Developed by EcoPlanet Bamboo, the project uses fast-growing bamboo as a high-carbon-density crop that can be commercially harvested for construction materials and bamboo products, providing both carbon sequestration and sustainable timber.

projects.name: Community Restoration of Native Ecosystems in Ghana
projects.description: Small-scale community reforestation project in Ghana restoring native forest ecosystems on degraded land through community-managed nurseries and planting programmes. The project works with local forest fringe communities to re-establish indigenous species on land degraded by logging, farming, and fire.

projects.name: Ghana Wli Agorviefe Afforestation Project
projects.description: Large-scale afforestation project in the Volta Region of Ghana establishing native tree species on severely degraded savanna land in the Wli-Agorviefe area near the Agumatsa Wildlife Sanctuary. Developed by Infused Seedball using direct seeding technology, the project restores forest cover on land depleted by fire management and agricultural expansion.

projects.name: Improved Agricultural Land Management for Verified Carbon Emission Reduction and Removal in Wa West and Daffiama-Bussie-Issah (DBI) Districts, Ghana
projects.description: Improved agricultural land management project across smallholder farming communities in Ghana applying soil carbon enhancement practices including composting, cover cropping, and agroforestry. Developed by Eco-Bliss Climate, the project works with smallholder farmers across multiple regions to shift from extractive to regenerative farming practices.

projects.name: Kwahu Landscape Restoration Project
projects.description: Forest landscape restoration project in the Kwahu Plateau of the Eastern Region of Ghana, targeting degraded farmland and degraded forest reserves for restoration through native tree planting. The project works in one of Ghana's most densely populated highland regions, restoring watershed forests critical for downstream water supply.

projects.name: Kwamisa/Other reserves community Forest Project
projects.description: Community forest management project protecting degraded forest reserves in the Kwamisa area of Ghana through community stewardship agreements. The project works with forest fringe communities to halt illegal logging and encroachment in classified forest reserves while providing alternative income through carbon credits.

projects.name: Nadowli-Kaleo Climate-Smart Agriculture Carbon Project
projects.description: Climate-smart agriculture project in the Upper West Region of Ghana working with smallholder farmers in the Nadowli-Kaleo district to adopt improved agricultural practices that build soil organic carbon. Developed by Axam Resources, the project combines agricultural carbon sequestration with improved yields for farmers in one of Ghana's most food-insecure regions.

projects.name: North Bandai Bamboo Reforestation Project, Ghana
projects.description: Bamboo reforestation project in northern Ghana establishing commercial bamboo plantations on degraded land adjacent to the Bandai Hills project area. Developed by EcoPlanet Bamboo, it forms part of a larger bamboo reforestation programme across the Brong-Ahafo Region combining commercial bamboo production with ecosystem restoration and carbon sequestration.

projects.name: Project Akwaaba | Forest Ecosystem Restoration in Ghana
projects.description: Small-scale forest ecosystem restoration project in Ghana reforesting degraded land with native rainforest species. The project name Akwaaba means welcome in Twi, reflecting the community-centred approach of working with local landholders to re-establish native forest cover on land degraded by slash-and-burn agriculture.

projects.name: Project Colobus | Forest Ecosystem Restoration In Eastern Ghana
projects.description: Forest restoration project in eastern Ghana establishing native forest species on degraded land to create habitat corridors for the western red colobus and other primates threatened by forest fragmentation. Developed by Rainforest Colobus, the project targets land between remaining forest fragments to reconnect isolated wildlife populations.

projects.name: Reforestation Of Degraded Forest Reserve Areas In Ghana, West Africa
projects.description: Commercial reforestation project in Ghana's High Forest Zone establishing timber and native tree species on degraded forest reserve land. Managed by Miro Forestry, the project plants fast-growing native species that provide both carbon sequestration and a sustainable commercial timber crop, reducing pressure on natural forests.

projects.name: Reforestation of Degraded Forest Reserves in Ghana
projects.description: Reforestation project in Ghana planting native species in degraded classified forest reserves in the High Forest Zone. Managed by Form Ghana, the project works within the formal forest reserve system to restore degraded compartments through commercial plantation forestry using a mix of timber and native species.

projects.name: Regenerative Development of Anlo Wetlands Project
projects.description: Wetland and mangrove restoration project in the Anlo wetlands of the Volta Region in southeastern Ghana, targeting degraded coastal wetland ecosystems on the eastern shore of the Volta River delta. Developed by Terraformation, the project restores mangrove cover and wetland vegetation in an area degraded by saltpan development and agricultural drainage.

projects.name: Balbuzard - Community-based mangrove conservation and restoration in Guinea
projects.description: Community-based blue carbon project protecting and restoring mangrove ecosystems in the Balbuzard area of coastal Guinea. Developed by Blue Coast Carbon, the project works with coastal fishing communities whose livelihoods depend on healthy mangrove habitat for fish, shrimp, and oysters. Guinea holds some of the most extensive and intact mangrove forests in West Africa.

projects.name: Community-Based Mangrove Conservation and Restoration in Guinea
projects.description: Blue carbon project protecting and restoring mangrove ecosystems in Guinea, working with coastal communities to halt mangrove clearance and restore degraded coastal habitat. Certified under VCS and generating 323K tCO2e/yr of carbon credits annually from mangrove conservation and reforestation.

projects.name: Lamantin - Community-based mangrove conservation and restoration in Guinea
projects.description: Community-based mangrove conservation project protecting coastal mangrove ecosystems in the Lamantin region of coastal Guinea. Developed by Blue Coast Carbon alongside the adjacent Balbuzard project, it forms part of a broader programme protecting Guineas internationally significant mangrove coast, which supports the West African manatee (lamantin) and other threatened species.

projects.name: Project Duiker | Forest Ecosystem Restoration in Eastern Guinea
projects.description: Forest restoration project in eastern Guinea restoring native rainforest on degraded land in the Upper Guinea Forest biodiversity hotspot, a region that has lost over 80% of its original forest cover. Developed by Rainforest Builder, the project targets land in forest transition zones to create habitat corridors for duiker antelope and other forest-dependent species.

projects.name: Yiri
projects.description: Community-based reforestation project in Guinea planting native tree species on degraded land across rural communities in the interior highlands. The project name Yiri means tree in Bambara, reflecting its community-led approach. It works with farming communities to integrate trees into agricultural landscapes through agroforestry and woodlot establishment.

projects.name: BissauBlue
projects.description: Blue carbon project protecting and restoring mangrove ecosystems in Guinea-Bissau, one of West Africa's most mangrove-rich nations with over 250,000 ha of intact coastal mangroves. Developed by Blue Forest, the project works with the Bijagos archipelago communities and mainland coastal villages to protect mangrove forests from clearance for rice paddy cultivation and charcoal production.

projects.name: Community Based Avoided Deforestation Project In Guinea-Bissau - Redd
projects.description: Community-based REDD+ project protecting tropical dry forest and humid forest ecosystems across multiple community forest management areas in Guinea-Bissau. Developed by the BioGuinea Foundation, the project works with rural communities to establish formal community forest governance structures that provide an alternative to clearance for subsistence farming.

projects.name: Conservation and Restoration of Classified Forests in Côte d’Ivoire
projects.description: Project protecting and restoring classified forest reserves in Ivory Coast, which has lost over 80% of its original forest cover since independence due to cocoa farming expansion. The project works within the formal forest reserve system to halt illegal agricultural encroachment and restore degraded forest compartments in a country that is the world's largest cocoa producer.

projects.name: Karidja forest restoration project
projects.description: Forest restoration project in Ivory Coast establishing native tree species on degraded land in the transition zone between the southern forest zone and the northern savanna. Developed by aDryada, the project restores forest cover in one of Africa's most deforested countries, targeting degraded land adjacent to remaining forest fragments to expand and connect protected areas.

projects.name: Agricentric Carbon Initiative
projects.description: Smallholder agricultural carbon project in Kenya improving soil health and carbon stocks through regenerative farming practices across small-scale farms in the central highlands and Rift Valley. Developed by Regenarative Carbon Alliance, the project works with farmer cooperatives to adopt practices including composting, mulching, and agroforestry that build soil organic carbon.

projects.name: Boomitra Carbon Farming in East Africa through Soil Enrichment
projects.description: Large-scale soil carbon project across smallholder farmland in Kenya and other East African countries, using Boomitras satellite and AI monitoring platform to measure soil carbon improvements from regenerative agriculture. The project works with hundreds of thousands of smallholder farmers to adopt cover cropping, reduced tillage, and composting practices verified remotely via soil spectroscopy.

projects.name: Boomitra Grassland Restoration in East Africa Through Soil Enrichment
projects.description: Grassland restoration and soil carbon project across degraded pasturelands in Kenya and the wider East African region. Developed by Boomitra, the project applies improved rangeland management practices including rotational grazing and grass restoration to rebuild soil organic carbon on degraded pastoral lands, using satellite remote sensing for monitoring.

projects.name: Chyulu Hills REDD+ Project
projects.description: Landmark REDD+ project protecting the Chyulu Hills forest and savanna ecosystem in southern Kenya, a critical water tower that feeds the Tsavo and Amboseli ecosystems. Developed by the Chyulu Hills Conservation Trust with Wildlife Works, the project protects Maasai community lands from agricultural encroachment and forest degradation, with carbon revenues shared with the local Maasai communities. The project area provides the primary water supply for Mombasa.

projects.name: Conservation Of Miombos In Taru  Kenya Redd+
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting miombo woodland in the Taru region of coastal Kenya from clearance for charcoal production and subsistence farming. The project works with local communities in the semi-arid Taru Desert zone to protect one of Kenya's least-studied dry forest ecosystems, which provides critical habitat for elephants and rare dry forest bird species.

projects.name: Entooma Sidai: A Maasai Mara Rangeland Restoration and Conservation Project
projects.description: Rangeland carbon project in the Maasai Mara ecosystem of southwestern Kenya working with Maasai landowners and group ranches to restore degraded pasturelands through rotational grazing management. The project targets rangelands that border the Maasai Mara National Reserve, reducing pressure on the reserve through improved land management on community lands.

projects.name: Kajiado Rangelands Carbon Project
projects.developer: Soils for the Future Africa Ltd
projects.description: Rangeland carbon project in Kajiado County, Kenya, working with Maasai pastoralist communities to restore degraded rangelands through improved herd management and rotational grazing. Developed by Soils for the Future Africa, the project applies the VM0032 soil carbon methodology to quantify and verify soil organic carbon improvements from changed grazing management across communal rangelands.

projects.name: Kenya Agricultural Carbon Project
projects.description: One of the first agricultural soil carbon projects in Africa, working with smallholder farmers in western Kenya and Tanzania through the Vi Agroforestry Programme to adopt sustainable land management practices. The project was a pioneering VCS agricultural carbon project that demonstrated the viability of soil carbon measurement on African smallholder farms.

projects.name: Kenya Climate Smart Agriculture Program
projects.description: Climate-smart agriculture programme in Kenya working with smallholder farmers to adopt improved farming practices that reduce emissions and build soil carbon. Developed by OYU Green, the project combines carbon sequestration with improved agricultural productivity, working with farmers across multiple counties in the central and western highlands.

projects.name: Kenya Highlands Grouped Carbon Project
projects.description: Grouped agricultural carbon project in the Kenyan highlands working with multiple smallholder farming groups to implement soil carbon improvement practices. The project aggregates small-scale farmers under a grouped VCS structure, making it financially viable to measure and verify carbon improvements at the smallholder level.

projects.name: Komaza Smallholder Farmer Forestry Kenya
projects.description: Smallholder forestry project in the Kilifi and Kwale districts of coastal Kenya working with subsistence farmers to establish tree lots as a commercial timber and carbon asset. Komaza provides trees, training, and market access, with farmers growing trees on 0.25 ha plots that provide income from timber sales and carbon credits. The model has been described as a micro-forestry franchise.

projects.name: Kuza Miti Agroforestry Carbon Project In Kenya
projects.description: Agroforestry carbon project in Kenya working with smallholder farmers to plant trees on their farmland through the Kuza Miti (grow trees in Swahili) programme. Developed by One Carbon World, the project supports farmers to integrate native and fruit tree species into food crop systems, providing shade, soil improvement, and timber while sequestering carbon.

projects.name: Lake Victoria Watershed Agroforestry Carbon Project
projects.description: Agroforestry carbon project in the Lake Victoria watershed of western Kenya working with smallholder farmers through Trees for the Future to integrate trees into farming systems. The project targets the highly degraded watershed landscapes around Lake Victoria, where tree planting improves soil fertility, reduces erosion, and provides fuelwood and timber while sequestering carbon.

projects.name: Livelihoods Mount Elgon Project
projects.description: Agroforestry project in the Mount Elgon region of western Kenya working with smallholder farming communities on the slopes of Mount Elgon to plant trees and restore degraded hillside farmland. Managed by the Livelihoods Fund, the project integrates tree planting with food crop production to improve farmer livelihoods and restore watershed function.

projects.name: Makueni Agroforestry Carbon Project
projects.description: Agroforestry carbon project in Makueni County in semi-arid southeastern Kenya working with smallholder farmers to plant drought-tolerant tree species on degraded farmland. The project targets one of Kenya's most food-insecure counties, where tree planting improves soil water retention, reduces soil erosion, and provides fuelwood and fodder.

projects.name: Misitu Arr Project - Mt. Kenya Region
projects.description: Large-scale reforestation project in the Mount Kenya region planting native forest species across degraded land on the slopes and foothills of Mount Kenya. The project name Misitu means forests in Swahili. Developed by AKILI Holdings, it works with landowners and communities around Mount Kenya to restore forest cover in one of East Africa's most critical water towers.

projects.name: Mount Kenya Regenerative Agroforestry Project
projects.description: Very small-scale pilot agroforestry project on the slopes of Mount Kenya integrating trees into smallholder farms on the mountain's lower slopes. The project demonstrates regenerative agroforestry approaches in a high-altitude equatorial mountain environment, providing a model for larger-scale forest restoration in the wider Mount Kenya ecosystem.

projects.name: Northern Kenya Grassland Carbon Project
projects.description: Flagship rangeland carbon project in the arid and semi-arid lands of northern Kenya working with pastoralist communities through the Northern Rangelands Trust to restore degraded rangelands through improved herd management. The project covers community conservancies across Samburu, Isiolo, Marsabit, and Laikipia counties, with carbon revenues supporting ranger salaries and community conservation activities.

projects.name: OYU - Reforesting Kenya for a better tomorrow
projects.description: Community reforestation project in Kenya planting native and commercial tree species on degraded land through a network of smallholder farmers and community groups. Developed by OYU Green, the project works across multiple counties to restore tree cover on degraded farmland and public land, combining carbon sequestration with timber production and improved water catchment.

projects.name: One Mara Carbon Project
projects.description: Rangeland carbon project on community lands adjacent to the Maasai Mara National Reserve in southwestern Kenya, working with Maasai group ranch members to improve rangeland condition through managed grazing. The project generates carbon credits from soil carbon improvements on community lands that serve as a critical wildlife corridor and buffer zone for the Mara ecosystem.

projects.name: Papariko - Restoration of Degraded Mangrove Areas in Kenya
projects.description: Mangrove restoration project in Kenya targeting degraded mangrove areas in coastal areas affected by shrimp farming, salt extraction, and fuelwood harvesting. Developed by Vlinder Austria, the project works with coastal communities to restore mangrove cover through community nurseries and replanting programmes, improving coastal protection and fishery habitat.

projects.name: Restore Africa: Restoring trees and livelihoods in Kenya
projects.description: Landscape restoration project in Kenya working with farming communities to plant trees and restore degraded land through the Global Evergreening Alliance's Restore Africa programme. The project applies farmer-managed natural regeneration (FMNR) alongside direct tree planting to rapidly increase tree cover on smallholder farms in drylands.

projects.name: TIST Program in Kenya, VCS 001
projects.description: One of the earliest entries in the TIST (Trees in the International Smallholder Tropics) programme in Kenya, establishing small-scale tree groves across smallholder farms in the central highlands. Clean Air Action Corporation developed the TIST model, which pays smallholder farmers directly for each tree they grow and maintain, using GPS-verified grove measurements.

projects.name: TIST Program in Kenya, VCS 002
projects.description: Second VCS-registered component of the TIST smallholder agroforestry programme in Kenya, expanding tree planting across smallholder farms in the Mount Kenya region. The TIST model uses simple GPS measurement and community-based monitoring to verify tree establishment and survival on thousands of small individual farm plots.

projects.name: TIST Program in Kenya, VCS 003
projects.description: Third registered component of the TIST smallholder agroforestry programme in Kenya, covering additional smallholder farm areas in the central highlands and Rift Valley. TIST is notable for paying farmers the majority of carbon revenues directly, with a transparent per-tree payment model that has made it one of Africa's most farmer-friendly carbon projects.

projects.name: TIST Program in Kenya, VCS 004
projects.description: Fourth component of the TIST smallholder programme in Kenya, expanding the model to additional farming communities through a network of small group clusters. Each TIST cluster of 6-12 farmers manages its grove portfolio collectively, receiving payments based on verified tree counts conducted by fellow farmers.

projects.name: TIST Program in Kenya, VCS 005
projects.description: One of the larger TIST programme components in Kenya, aggregating thousands of smallholder farmer groves across the central highlands, western Kenya, and Rift Valley. The TIST programme in Kenya has enrolled over 80,000 farmers and planted tens of millions of trees, making it one of the largest smallholder agroforestry programmes on the continent.

projects.name: TIST Program in Kenya, VCS 006
projects.description: Large-scale TIST smallholder agroforestry programme component in Kenya, covering a significant portion of the programme's total grove portfolio across multiple counties. The component includes both native forest species and commercial timber trees grown in small grove lots on individual smallholder farms.

projects.name: TIST Program in Kenya, VCS 009
projects.description: Additional TIST programme component in Kenya expanding coverage across smallholder farming communities in the Rift Valley and western Kenya regions. The TIST model has been replicated across Uganda, Tanzania, and India, but Kenya remains its largest and most established programme country.

projects.name: TIST Program in Kenya, VCS-011
projects.description: Expanded TIST programme component in Kenya, one of the largest individual VCS registrations in the TIST portfolio, covering a substantial portion of the programme's smallholder grove network across Kenya's agricultural zones. CCB certification reflects the programme's strong community co-benefits including farmer payments, group savings, and agricultural training.

projects.name: TIST Program in Kenya, VCS-CCB 010
projects.description: TIST smallholder agroforestry programme component in Kenya registered under the AR-AMS0007 methodology with CCB certification, reflecting the programme's strong community and biodiversity co-benefits. The CCB Gold certification recognises the programmes exceptional community benefit-sharing model and biodiversity outcomes from diverse native species planting.

projects.name: The Kasigau Corridor REDD Project - Phase II The Community Ranches
projects.description: Phase II of the pioneering Kasigau Corridor REDD+ project, covering a network of community ranches in Taita-Taveta County, southern Kenya that form a critical wildlife corridor between Tsavo East and Tsavo West National Parks. Developed by Wildlife Works Carbon, Phase II expanded the project to include six community ranches covering over 169,000 ha, with carbon revenues funding a community-owned factory employing over 2,000 women garment workers.

projects.name: The Kasigau Corridor REDD Project – Phase I Rukinga Sanctuary
projects.description: The first Phase of the Kasigau Corridor REDD+ project and one of the first large-scale REDD+ projects in Africa to generate and sell verified carbon credits. The Rukinga Sanctuary component covers a private ranch that serves as the core of the Kasigau wildlife corridor between Tsavo East and West, protecting habitat for elephants, cheetahs, and endangered Hirolas. Developed by Wildlife Works Carbon, it became a global model for community-based REDD+.

projects.name: Tukuze
projects.description: Reforestation project in Kenya planting native and commercial tree species on degraded land through the ETG Climate Solutions platform, working with rural farming communities across central and western Kenya. The project name Tukuze means let us grow in Swahili, reflecting the community-based approach of working with local farmers to establish trees on their land.

projects.name: Western Kenya Soil Carbon Project
projects.description: Soil carbon project in western Kenya working with smallholder farmers to improve soil organic carbon through improved agricultural practices. One of the earliest African agricultural carbon projects under the VCS VM0017 methodology, the project works with farming cooperatives in Siaya and Kisumu counties to measure and verify soil carbon improvements.

projects.name: Wonegizi REDD+ Pilot Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting tropical rainforest in the Wonegizi Proposed Protected Area in Lofa County, northwestern Liberia, part of the Upper Guinea Forest biodiversity hotspot. Developed by Fauna and Flora International under the new VM0048 methodology, the project works with local communities to protect one of Liberia's last intact forest landscapes, which is home to chimpanzees, pygmy hippos, and forest elephants.

projects.name: Ala Honko Blue Carbon Madagascar
projects.description: Blue carbon project protecting and restoring mangrove ecosystems along the southwestern coast of Madagascar in the Ala Honko (meaning mangrove in Malagasy) project area. The project works with coastal fishing communities to halt mangrove clearance, with carbon revenues funding community conservation and alternative livelihood programmes.

projects.name: Beampingaratsy REDD Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting humid forest in the Beampingaratsy protected area in southeastern Madagascar, a biodiversity hotspot containing endemic lemur species and unique dry spiny forest ecosystems. The project works with the BNC REDD+ organisation to halt deforestation driven by slash-and-burn agriculture and charcoal production.

projects.name: Carbon Emissions Reduction Project in the Corridor Ankeniheny-Zahamena (CAZ) Protected Area, Madagascar
projects.developer: Govt. of Madagascar, Direction Generale des Forets
projects.description: Large-scale REDD+ project protecting the Ankeniheny-Zahamena Corridor, one of Madagascar's largest remaining continuous tracts of eastern humid forest and a UNESCO natural heritage site. Managed by the Malagasy government through the Direction Generale des Forets, the CAZ corridor connects two major national parks and protects habitat for dozens of lemur species and thousands of endemic plants.

projects.name: Carbon Emissions Reduction Project in the Forest Corridor Ambositra-Vondrozo (COFAV), Madagascar
projects.developer: Govt. of Madagascar, Direction Generale des Forets
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting the Ambositra-Vondrozo forest corridor (COFAV) in the central highlands of Madagascar, connecting fragmented humid forest patches in a heavily deforested landscape. Managed by the Malagasy government, the project protects habitat for the critically endangered greater bamboo lemur and Malagasy giant jumping rat.

projects.name: Fagnako: Agroforestry project in Madagascar
projects.description: Agroforestry project in central Madagascar integrating trees into smallholder farming systems to restore degraded hillside land and reduce pressure on remaining natural forest. Developed by Canopy Energies (unrelated to the Canopy platform), the project promotes shade-grown vanilla and other cash crops under native tree cover in one of Madagascar's most deforested highland regions.

projects.name: Reforestation of deforested land in Madagascar
projects.description: Reforestation project in Madagascar planting native species on deforested land to restore degraded landscapes. Madagascar has lost over 90% of its original forest cover and continues to experience high deforestation rates, making reforestation one of the most critical conservation interventions in one of the worlds most biodiverse nations.

projects.name: Satrokala Arr Grouped Project
projects.description: Grouped reforestation project in Madagascar aggregating multiple small-scale community tree planting activities into a single VCS project. Developed by JTF Madagascar, the Satrokala (meaning place where pines grow) project works with farming communities in the central highlands to establish native and productive tree species on degraded savanna and fallow land.

projects.name: Teraka Madagascar: Smallholder Tree Planting Program
projects.description: Smallholder tree planting programme in Madagascar working with subsistence farming families to plant trees on their land through the ITERAKA social enterprise. The project provides trees, training, and carbon payment to smallholder families in one of the world's poorest countries, combining carbon sequestration with improved fuelwood supply and soil fertility.

projects.name: The Makira Forest Protected Area in Madagascar
projects.description: Landmark REDD+ project protecting the Makira Natural Park in northeastern Madagascar, one of the country's largest protected areas and a global biodiversity hotspot containing over 50 lemur species and 2,000 endemic plant species. Managed by the Wildlife Conservation Society, carbon revenues fund the park management, anti-poaching patrols, and community development across 41 villages in one of Madagascar's most remote and biodiverse regions.

projects.name: failed
projects.description: Failed or inactive project record in the Madagascar portfolio.

projects.name: Agricultural Land Management in Malawi
projects.description: Agricultural land management project in Malawi working with smallholder farmers to improve soil carbon through better farming practices. Developed by Teralytic using soil sensor technology to monitor carbon changes, the project applies improved practices including cover cropping, reduced tillage, and compost application across smallholder plots in central Malawi.

projects.name: Bamboo Afforestation in Malawi
projects.developer: C-Quest Capital SG Africa Holdings Private Limited
projects.description: Bamboo afforestation project in Malawi establishing bamboo plantations on degraded land to provide sustainable fuelwood and construction materials as alternatives to natural forest wood. Managed by C-Quest Capital, the project reduces deforestation pressure by providing communities with a fast-growing renewable source of biomass, while sequestering significant carbon in bamboo biomass and soil.

projects.name: Fuelling a greener future for farmers in Malawi through the use of Jatropha curcas
projects.description: Bioenergy project in Malawi establishing jatropha plantations on marginal land to provide a renewable fuel source for rural communities. The project works with smallholder farmers to plant jatropha on non-food-crop land, with seeds processed into oil for cooking and lighting, reducing dependence on charcoal and kerosene.

projects.name: Kulera Landscape REDD+ Program for Co-Managed Protected Areas, Malawi
projects.developer: Malawi Department of Forestry / community partners
projects.description: REDD+ landscape programme across co-managed protected areas in northern Malawi, protecting afromontane forest and miombo woodland in a network of forest reserves and wildlife management areas. The programme works with the Malawi Department of Forestry and local communities to reduce deforestation while generating carbon revenues for conservation finance.

projects.name: MoRe Malawi ARR Project
projects.description: Large-scale reforestation project across Malawi planting native tree species on degraded land through a network of smallholder farmers and community groups. Developed by Mamaland Company, the project aims to restore significant areas of degraded miombo woodland and hillside farmland across multiple districts in one of Africa's most densely populated and deforested countries.

projects.name: MoRe Malawi REDD Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting remaining natural forest and woodland in Malawi from clearance for charcoal and subsistence farming. Developed by Mamaland Company alongside the MORE Malawi ARR project, it protects existing forest while the ARR project actively restores degraded land, together forming a comprehensive landscape conservation approach.

projects.name: One Acre Fund Munda Otsogola Program
projects.description: Agroforestry programme in Malawi working through One Acre Fund's established farmer network to distribute trees to smallholder farming families. Munda Otsogola means may your farm yield abundantly in Chichewa. The programme integrates tree planting into One Acre Fund's existing agricultural input loan and training programme, reaching hundreds of thousands of farmers.

projects.name: Regenerative Agriculture and Forestry Technologies (RAFT)
projects.description: Integrated reforestation and regenerative agriculture project in Malawi combining tree planting with improved farming practices to restore degraded land and build soil carbon. Developed by Bridge Carbon, the RAFT programme works with smallholder farmers to adopt agroforestry and soil restoration techniques that improve agricultural yields while sequestering carbon.

projects.name: Regenerative Agriculture and Forestry Technologies (RAFT) 1
projects.developer: C-Quest Capital SG Africa Holdings Private Limited
projects.description: Agricultural land management component of the RAFT programme in Malawi, working with smallholder farmers to improve soil organic carbon through improved farming practices. Managed by C-Quest Capital, this component applies the VM0042 methodology to measure and verify soil carbon improvements from changed agricultural management on smallholder plots.

projects.name: Smallholder Reforestation in Malawi
projects.description: Small-scale smallholder reforestation project in Malawi working with individual farming families to plant trees on their land. The project supports farmers to establish tree nurseries and plant native species on degraded hillside land and farm boundaries, providing fuelwood and timber while improving watershed function.

projects.name: Bougouni-Yanfolila Forest Project, Mali
projects.description: Reforestation project in the Bougouni-Yanfolila area of southern Mali, one of the wetter zones of the Sahel, restoring native tree species on degraded savanna and farmland in the sub-Sudanian zone. The project works with rural farming communities to establish woodlots and agroforestry systems that provide fuelwood and building materials while sequestering carbon.

projects.name: Jatropha Curcas grouped project in Mali
projects.description: Very small-scale jatropha bioenergy project in Mali establishing jatropha plantations on marginal degraded land to provide a renewable fuel source for rural communities. The extremely low credit volume of 3 tCO2e/yr indicates this is a pilot demonstration project working with a small number of participating households.

projects.name: Project Tourba - Adoption Of Improved Agricultural Practices Towards Carbon Farming In Morocco
projects.description: Very large agricultural carbon project in Morocco working with smallholder farmers across multiple agro-climatic zones to adopt improved farming practices that build soil organic carbon. Developed by InnovX, Tourba applies the VM0017 soil carbon methodology across a massive portfolio of smallholder farms, making it one of the largest agricultural carbon projects ever registered.

projects.name: Tafilalet Agroforestry System  Morocco
projects.description: Agroforestry project in the Tafilalet oasis region of southeastern Morocco, integrating date palms, fruit trees, and native species into traditional oasis farming systems. The project works with oasis farming communities in a UNESCO-recognised biosphere reserve to restore traditional multi-strata garden systems that sequester carbon while sustaining the agro-pastoral heritage of the pre-Saharan oases.

projects.name: Agri-SMART: Sustaining a resilient and inclusive development in Zambezia
projects.description: Agricultural land management project in the Zambezia Province of Mozambique working with smallholder farmers to adopt improved farming practices that build soil carbon and resilience. Developed by Carbon Sink Group, the project applies the VM0017 methodology to measure soil carbon improvements from changed management on smallholder plots in one of Mozambiques most populated provinces.

projects.name: Gilé National Reserve REDD Project
projects.developer: National Mozambican Public Agency for Parks and Reserves Conservation (ANAC)
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting the Gilé National Reserve in Zambezia Province, Mozambique, a protected area covering miombo woodland and rare coastal forest ecosystems. Managed by ANAC, Mozambique's national parks agency, the project protects habitat for sable antelope, elephant, and rare endemic species while generating carbon revenues to fund reserve management.

projects.name: Mozblue: Building Africa’S Largest Mangrove Restoration Project
projects.description: Blue carbon project developing what could become Africa's largest mangrove restoration project along the coast of Mozambique, targeting degraded mangrove systems in the Zambezia and Nampula provinces. Developed by Blue Forest Mozambique, the project works with coastal fishing communities to restore mangrove habitat critical for fish nurseries and coastal protection.

projects.name: Revegetation with fruit trees in North Manica Province, Mozambique
projects.description: Reforestation project in Manica Province of central Mozambique planting fruit trees on degraded agricultural land as part of an integrated land restoration and food security programme. Developed by Agrimoz, the project uses fruit tree species that provide both carbon sequestration and direct nutritional and income benefits for farming families.

projects.name: Rovuma Belt
projects.developer: Fundação para o Desenvolvimento de Palma
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting miombo woodland in the Rovuma Belt region of northern Mozambique, adjacent to the Tanzanian border, where large-scale agricultural conversion threatens one of Africa's most intact miombo landscapes. The project works with rural communities along the Rovuma River to protect woodland and earn income from carbon credits.

projects.name: Yambone REDD+ Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting miombo woodland and forest in the Yambone concession area of Mozambique from agricultural conversion and charcoal production. Managed by Carbonsink Mozambique, the project works with communities in a remote area of central Mozambique to halt deforestation through conservation agreements and alternative livelihood programmes.

projects.name: Regeneration of the Kunene Highlands Elephant Landscape, Namibia
projects.description: Rangeland restoration project in the Kunene Region of northwestern Namibia working with communal conservancies and elephant conservation initiatives to restore degraded pasturelands through improved herd management. The project targets the elephant landscape that forms a critical corridor for desert-adapted elephants between Namibia and Angola.

projects.name: Niger Acacia Senegal Plantation Project
projects.description: Afforestation project in Niger establishing Acacia senegal (gum arabic) plantations on degraded Sahelian land as part of the broader Great Green Wall initiative. Gum arabic production provides a direct commercial income for farming communities, making the project financially sustainable while restoring tree cover in one of the world's most climate-vulnerable regions.

projects.name: Niger Acacia Senegal Plantation Project - CER Conversion
projects.description: VCS conversion of a previously CDM-registered Acacia senegal plantation project in Niger, transferring the existing project into the VCS registry to access voluntary carbon markets. The project maintains the same plantation area and management approach as the original CDM project.

projects.name: Carbon Mosaic Conservation and Restoration of Mangroves in Delta State
projects.description: Large-scale mangrove conservation and restoration project in the Niger Delta of Delta State, Nigeria, protecting and restoring mangrove ecosystems in one of Africa's most oil-polluted and ecologically degraded coastal regions. The project works with Niger Delta communities to restore mangrove cover and carbon stocks in a region where oil spills and illegal artisanal refining have devastated coastal ecosystems.

projects.name: Gashaka Gumti Forest Carbon Project
projects.description: Reforestation project in and around the Gashaka Gumti National Park in Taraba State, Nigeria's largest national park and home to chimpanzees, lions, and Nigeria's highest peak. Developed by Africa Nature Investors, the project restores degraded buffer zone land through native tree planting while supporting the long-term financial sustainability of the park through carbon revenues.

projects.name: Jaji ARR Project
projects.description: Small-scale reforestation project in the Jaji area of Kaduna State, Nigeria, establishing native tree species on degraded farmland. The project works with smallholder farmers to integrate trees into their agricultural systems, providing fuelwood and shade while sequestering carbon in one of Nigeria's most deforested highland zones.

projects.name: Nigeria Community-based Forestry Project
projects.description: Community-based reforestation project in Nigeria working with farming communities to establish trees on degraded land through a participatory forestry model. The project aggregates multiple small community planting activities across several states into a single VCS project, making carbon finance accessible to communities planting at small scale.

projects.name: Tourba Project – Improved Agriculture Practices In Nigeria
projects.description: Agricultural land management project in Nigeria working with smallholder farmers to adopt improved farming practices that build soil organic carbon. Developed by Tourba Carbon Farming Nigeria, the project applies the VM0042 methodology to measure soil carbon improvements from practices including composting, cover cropping, and agroforestry across a large portfolio of smallholder farms.

projects.name: Agroforestry plantation Bateke Plateau, Republic of Congo
projects.description: Agroforestry plantation project on the Bateke Plateau of the Republic of Congo, establishing native tree species and agroforestry systems on the vast degraded grasslands of this elevated plateau region. Developed by FRM Commitment, the project restores tree cover in a landscape that was formerly forested but has been degraded by repeated burning and agricultural land use.

projects.name: BaCaSi
projects.description: Large-scale reforestation project in the Republic of Congo developed by TotalEnergies Nature Based Solutions establishing native and commercial tree species on degraded land. The project is part of TotalEnergies carbon neutrality programme and represents one of the largest corporate-sponsored reforestation investments in Central Africa.

projects.name: Conkouati-Douli National Park REDD+ Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting the Conkouati-Douli National Park on the coastal Atlantic zone of the Republic of Congo, a UNESCO biosphere reserve protecting lowland tropical forest, savanna, and mangrove ecosystems. Managed by the NGO Noe, the project protects habitat for forest elephants, western lowland gorillas, and one of Africa's last remaining wild populations of the critically endangered leatherback sea turtle.

projects.name: Improved Forest Management Through Logged-To-Protected Forests And Reduced Impact Logging In Ufa Ngombé, Republic Of Congo
projects.description: Improved forest management project in the Republic of Congo transitioning a commercial logging concession to a fully protected conservation zone while applying reduced-impact logging in adjoining production forests. Managed by Interholco, one of Congo's major timber companies, the project represents a significant commercial forestry pivot toward conservation-compatible forest management.

projects.name: Liza Protect Forest
projects.description: Forest protection project in the Republic of Congo protecting intact rainforest from clearance and degradation through REDD+ carbon financing. Developed by CARBON13, the project works in a forest landscape in the northern Congo Basin where illegal logging and agricultural encroachment are the primary deforestation drivers.

projects.name: North Pikounda REDD+
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting the North Pikounda forest concession in the Sangha Region of northern Republic of Congo, adjacent to the Nouabale-Ndoki National Park and part of the Sangha Trinational UNESCO World Heritage landscape. The project halts logging in a forest concession to create a permanent conservation zone protecting forest elephants, western lowland gorillas, and bongo antelope.

projects.name: Oka 2 Project
projects.description: Small-scale reforestation project on the Bateke Plateau of the Republic of Congo establishing native Acacia and forest tree species on degraded savanna grassland. Developed by Acacia Bateke Capital, it forms a component of a broader programme of afforestation on the plateau's degraded grasslands.

projects.name: The Gamboma and Mbon Afforestation Project
projects.description: Very large reforestation project on the Bateke Plateau of the Republic of Congo establishing native tree species and mixed forest on the vast degraded grasslands of this elevated plateau region. Developed by EcoNetix GmbH, the Gamboma and Mbon areas represent a major planting programme that could transform significant areas of degraded savanna back to native forest.

projects.name: The Massangui Forest Conservation and Afforestation Project
projects.description: Combined forest conservation and afforestation project in the Massangui area of the Republic of Congo, protecting existing forest while actively planting trees on adjacent degraded land. Developed by EcoNetix GmbH, the project uses carbon finance to both prevent deforestation and expand forest cover in a landscape under pressure from agricultural expansion.

projects.name: The Republic of the Congo Improved Forest Management Project
projects.description: Very large improved forest management project in a commercial timber concession in the Republic of Congo, introducing reduced-impact logging and a network of protected forest set-asides within an active timber operation. Managed by the Société d'Exploitation Forestiere Yuan Dong, the project represents one of the largest IFM projects ever registered globally.

projects.name: Agroforestry For Livelihoods
projects.description: Community agroforestry project in Rwanda working with smallholder farmers across the country's densely populated hillsides to integrate trees into food crop systems. Managed by the Livelihoods Fund, the project plants native and productive tree species on farm boundaries and degraded hillside land, improving soil fertility, providing fuelwood, and generating carbon credits.

projects.name: Agroforestry For Livelihoods Project
projects.description: Follow-on component of the Rwanda Livelihoods agroforestry programme, expanding tree planting activities to additional farming communities across Rwanda's intensively farmed hillside landscapes. Both components work through the Livelihoods Fund's established community network to scale up agroforestry adoption across one of Africa's most densely populated countries.

projects.name: East Africa Soil and Biodiversity improvement project
projects.description: Agricultural soil carbon and biodiversity project in Rwanda and other East African countries working with smallholder farmers through the eAgronom digital agriculture platform. The project applies improved farming practices verified via the platform's digital monitoring system to build soil organic carbon on smallholder plots.

projects.name: Rwanda Riparian Restoration Project
projects.description: Riparian forest restoration project in Rwanda establishing bamboo and native tree species along stream banks and valley bottoms to restore degraded riparian corridors across the country's heavily farmed hill slopes. Developed by EcoPlanet Bamboo, the project protects water catchments and reduces soil erosion while sequestering carbon in riparian vegetation.

projects.name: Tubura Forest Landscape Restoration Program
projects.description: Forest landscape restoration programme in Rwanda working through One Acre Fund's Tubura farmer network to plant trees on smallholder farms across the country. The programme distributes trees to hundreds of thousands of farming families through One Acre Fund's existing distribution network, integrating tree planting into the Fund's agricultural support model.

projects.name: ALLCOT BLUE CARBON MANGROVE PROJECT IN SENEGAL (ABC  Mangrove Senegal)
projects.description: Blue carbon project protecting and restoring mangrove ecosystems in Senegal developed by Allcot, one of Africa's leading carbon project developers. The project works with coastal fishing communities in the Casamance and Sine-Saloum regions to halt mangrove clearance and restore degraded mangrove areas.

projects.name: Community-based Mangrove Restoration Initiative – Lep Lep
projects.description: Community-based mangrove restoration project in Senegal working with coastal village communities to restore degraded mangrove stands in the Sine-Saloum delta and Casamance regions. Developed by Vlinder Austria, the project uses community nurseries and traditional planting knowledge to re-establish mangrove cover in areas cleared for salt pans and rice cultivation.

projects.name: Forêts d'Avenir
projects.description: Reforestation project in southern Senegal planting native tree species in the Casamance region through a community forestry model. The name Forts d'Avenir (Forests of the Future) reflects the long-term vision of restoring forest cover in the Casamance, which has one of Senegal's highest remaining forest cover but faces pressure from charcoal production and agricultural expansion.

projects.name: Jatropha Agroforestry Senegal
projects.description: Small-scale agroforestry project in Senegal integrating jatropha plants into farming systems as a bioenergy crop. The extremely low credit volume indicates this is a very small pilot project, likely demonstrating the carbon sequestration potential of jatropha-based agroforestry systems at community scale.

projects.name: Livelihoods' mangrove restoration grouped project in Senegal
projects.description: Mangrove restoration programme in Senegal managed by the Livelihoods Fund, working with coastal communities across the Sine-Saloum delta and Casamance to restore degraded mangrove ecosystems. The Livelihoods Fund pioneered mangrove carbon projects in West Africa and the Senegal programme is one of its flagship community restoration initiatives.

projects.name: Mangrove Restoration Project with Sine Saloum and Casamance communities, Senegal
projects.description: Community mangrove restoration project in the Sine-Saloum delta and Casamance regions of Senegal, working with traditional fishing communities to restore mangrove ecosystems degraded by saltwater intrusion, hypersalinity, and clearance. Managed by WeForest, the project applies traditional community-based replanting techniques alongside active nursery propagation.

projects.name: Niokolo Koba REDD+ Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting the Niokolo-Koba National Park ecosystem in southeastern Senegal, a UNESCO World Heritage Site that is the largest protected area in West Africa and home to the last lions and chimpanzees in Senegal. The project works with buffer zone communities to reduce pressure on the park's woodland and savanna habitats.

projects.name: Senegal And West Africa Mangrove Programme (Swamp)
projects.description: Small blue carbon project in Senegal as part of a broader West Africa mangrove programme, protecting and restoring mangrove ecosystems across coastal Senegal. Managed by Allcot, this component forms part of the wider SWAMP programme that addresses mangrove conservation across multiple West African countries.

projects.name: Tamba 20000
projects.description: Reforestation project in the Tambacounda region of eastern Senegal establishing 20,000 ha of native tree species on degraded Sahelian land. Developed by Hommes et Terre Global, the project works with rural communities in one of Senegal's hottest and most climate-vulnerable regions to restore tree cover through community-managed planting.

projects.name: The Haidar EL ALI Mangrove Initiative (HEAMI)
projects.description: Community mangrove restoration initiative in Senegal named for the leading Senegalese environmental activist Haidar El Ali, former environment minister and founder of the Oceanium conservation NGO. The project applies the community-based mangrove restoration methods pioneered by Oceanium across the coastal communities of the Casamance and Sine-Saloum.

projects.name: Community-Based Mangrove Conservation and Restoration in Sierra Leone
projects.description: Community-based blue carbon project protecting and restoring mangrove ecosystems across the extensive mangrove coast of Sierra Leone, working with coastal fishing communities to halt clearance for rice cultivation and charcoal. Sierra Leone has some of West Africa's most significant remaining mangrove forests, and this project seeks to protect them through community carbon finance.

projects.name: Gola REDD project
projects.description: Flagship REDD+ project protecting the Gola Rainforest National Park in southeastern Sierra Leone, part of the Upper Guinea Forest biodiversity hotspot and home to pygmy hippos, chimpanzees, and over 300 bird species. Managed by the Gola Rainforest Conservation and the Royal Society for the Protection of Birds, the project demonstrates how sustainable conservation finance can protect a critical national park while benefiting surrounding communities.

projects.name: Improved Agricultural Land Management in Sierra Leone
projects.description: Agricultural land management project in Sierra Leone working with smallholder farmers to improve soil organic carbon through better farming practices. Developed by Teralytic using advanced soil monitoring technology, the project applies the VM0042 methodology to measure carbon improvements from changed management on smallholder plots.

projects.name: Mangrove Conservation Sierra Leone
projects.description: Mangrove conservation and restoration project along the extensive coastline of Sierra Leone, protecting mangrove ecosystems from clearance for rice farming and mangrove timber harvesting. Developed by Mangrove Conservation Sierra Leone, the project engages coastal communities in the Northern Province and Western Area in mangrove stewardship agreements.

projects.name: Project Buffalo | Western Guinean Lowland Forest Ecosystem Restoration
projects.description: Forest ecosystem restoration project in Sierra Leone targeting the Western Guinean Lowland Forest ecoregion, one of the most biodiverse and most threatened forest ecosystems in West Africa. Developed by Rainforest Builder, the project plants native forest species on degraded land to create habitat corridors for the buffalo and other forest mammals.

projects.name: Reforestation of Degraded Lands in Sierra Leone
projects.description: Commercial reforestation project in Sierra Leone establishing timber and native tree species on degraded land in the forest zone. Managed by Miro Forestry, the project plants a mixture of fast-growing timber species and native hardwoods on degraded land, providing both carbon sequestration and a long-term commercial timber resource.

projects.name: Rewilding Maforki
projects.description: Forest rewilding project in the Maforki chiefdom of northern Sierra Leone restoring degraded forest landscape through natural regeneration and strategic planting of native species. Developed by Rewilding Maforki, the project targets land degraded by charcoal production and shifting cultivation in the transition zone between the forest and savanna zones.

projects.name: Scarcies Mangrove Rewilding Project
projects.description: Mangrove restoration project in the Great and Little Scarcies estuaries of northern Sierra Leone, restoring degraded mangrove ecosystems in one of West Africa's most productive estuarine systems. The project works with fishing communities in the Scarcies estuaries to restore mangroves through assisted natural regeneration and active planting.

projects.name: Sierra Leone PRIDO ARR-Agroforestry Program
projects.description: Agroforestry and reforestation programme across multiple communities in Sierra Leone integrating trees into farming systems and restoring degraded land through direct planting. The project works with rural communities across several provinces to adopt agroforestry as a sustainable land use that improves farm productivity while sequestering carbon.

projects.name: Baviaanskloof Carbon Project
projects.description: Thicket restoration and reforestation project in the Baviaanskloof Megareserve of the Eastern Cape, South Africa, restoring degraded spekboom (Portulacaria afra) thicket through targeted replanting and natural regeneration. Managed by the Baviaanskloof Bewarea NPC, the project works in one of South Africa's most biodiverse protected areas, a UNESCO World Heritage Site that forms part of the Cape Floristic Region.

projects.name: CNG AgriCarbon Rewards Programme
projects.description: Agricultural soil carbon programme in South Africa working with commercial and small-scale farmers to build soil organic carbon through regenerative farming practices. Developed by Anthesis, the programme applies the VM0042 methodology to verify soil carbon improvements from practices including cover cropping, reduced tillage, and composting.

projects.name: CNG AgriCarbon Rewards Programme 2
