projects.description: Expanded second phase of the South Africa agricultural carbon programme, scaling up soil carbon verification across a larger portfolio of farming operations. Managed by Climate Neutral Group, this component applies the VM0042 methodology at significant scale across commercial farming operations in multiple provinces.

projects.name: CSA Carbon
projects.description: Small reforestation and thicket restoration project in the Western Cape of South Africa establishing native fynbos and forest species on degraded agricultural land. Developed by C-SA Properties, the project restores land in the Cape Floristic Region, one of the world's six floral kingdoms and a major global biodiversity hotspot.

projects.name: Carbon Conservancy – Farms West Of Musina (Plot 1: Limpopo Safaris), South Africa
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting bush and savanna vegetation on private game farm land west of Musina in the Limpopo Province of South Africa, preventing the conversion of bushveld habitat to agriculture. The project protects the natural vegetation of a private wildlife reserve adjacent to the Limpopo National Park transfrontier conservation area.

projects.name: Cultivating Carbon Regenerative Agriculture Project
projects.description: Regenerative agriculture carbon project in South Africa working with commercial grain and livestock farmers to improve soil health and carbon stocks through farming practice changes. Developed by Cultivating Carbon, the project applies reduced tillage, cover crops, and pasture restoration to build soil organic carbon on commercial farming operations.

projects.name: Eastern Cape Bamboo Forestry Project, South Africa
projects.description: Commercial bamboo plantation project in the Eastern Cape of South Africa establishing bamboo on degraded agricultural and marginal land. Managed by EcoPlanet Bamboo, the project creates a commercial bamboo forestry operation that provides construction materials and bioenergy as alternatives to timber, while sequestering significant carbon in fast-growing bamboo biomass.

projects.name: Eastern Cape Restoration Project, South Africa - Makhanda
projects.description: Native vegetation and bamboo restoration project near Makhanda (Grahamstown) in the Eastern Cape, restoring degraded land with native species and bamboo under the EcoPlanet Bamboo programme. The project targets historically farmed land in the Eastern Cape highlands for transition back to native forest and bamboo.

projects.name: Eastern Cape Restoration Project, South Africa - Somerset East
projects.description: Bamboo and native species restoration project near Somerset East in the Eastern Cape Karoo-Highlands transition zone, restoring degraded farmland under the EcoPlanet Bamboo programme. The project uses bamboo as a pioneer species on severely degraded land before transitioning to native vegetation, sequestering significant carbon in the rapidly growing bamboo.

projects.name: Grassland Restoration and Stewardship in South Africa (GRASS)
projects.description: Grassland conservation and restoration project in South Africa protecting and restoring natural grassland habitats, which are among the most threatened ecosystems in the country. Grasslands are critical for soil carbon storage and water catchment but have been severely degraded by overgrazing and conversion to cropland and pine plantations.

projects.name: Green Mpumalanga Project (GMP)
projects.description: Rangeland carbon project in Mpumalanga Province, South Africa, improving soil carbon through better grassland and rangeland management on commercial and communal farming land. Developed by Regoapele Capital, the project applies improved grazing management to restore degraded grasslands in the Highveld and Escarpment zones of Mpumalanga.

projects.name: Hive Ecosystems project
projects.description: Reforestation project in South Africa establishing native tree species on degraded agricultural land through a network of small community and private landowner planting sites. Developed by HIVE Ecosystems, the project aggregates multiple small planting sites into a single VCS grouped project, making carbon finance accessible to small-scale landowners.

projects.name: Karoo Sustainable Landscapes Program (KSLP)
projects.description: Very large rangeland carbon programme in the Karoo region of the Northern and Western Cape of South Africa, working with commercial livestock farmers to restore degraded karoo vegetation through improved grazing management. Developed by Sayari Earth, the programme applies the VM0032 methodology across the vast karoo semi-desert, a unique biome with extremely high endemism.

projects.name: Kruger to Canyons Rangeland Restoration Project
projects.description: Rangeland restoration project in the Kruger to Canyons UNESCO Biosphere Reserve of Limpopo Province, South Africa, working with communal and small-scale farmers to restore degraded grassland and bushveld through improved grazing management. Developed by Conservation South Africa, the project improves rangelands in the buffer zone of the Kruger National Park.

projects.name: Kuzuko Lodge Private Game Reserve thicket restoration project
projects.description: Thicket and spekboom restoration project on Kuzuko Lodge private game reserve in the Eastern Cape, restoring degraded valley bushveld through targeted replanting of Portulacaria afra (spekboom). Spekboom is one of the world's most carbon-dense plant species, capable of storing up to 4 tonnes of CO2 per hectare per year in the right conditions.

projects.name: KwaZulu-Natal Sustainable Landscapes Program (KZNSLP)
projects.description: Large-scale rangeland and grassland carbon programme in KwaZulu-Natal Province, South Africa, working with commercial and communal farmers to restore degraded grassland through improved land management. Developed by Sayari Earth as a companion programme to the Karoo programme, it targets the KwaZulu-Natal grasslands which are among South Africa's most biodiverse yet threatened ecosystems.

projects.name: Orizon CarbonCrop Rewards Programme
projects.description: Agricultural soil carbon programme in South Africa working with commercial crop farmers to build soil organic carbon through regenerative practices including cover crops, reduced tillage, and organic amendments. Developed by Orizon Agriculture, the programme applies the VM0042 methodology to measure and verify soil carbon improvements on commercial grain farming operations.

projects.name: Peri-urban bamboo planting around South African townships
projects.description: Community bamboo planting project establishing bamboo stands around South African townships to provide a sustainable source of fuelwood and construction materials. The project targets peri-urban communities where residents are highly dependent on fuelwood, with bamboo providing a fast-growing renewable alternative to natural woodland charcoal.

projects.name: Portland: Regenerative Agriculture & native revegetation on degraded land
projects.description: Very small-scale regenerative agriculture and native revegetation project on degraded land in South Africa, demonstrating the carbon sequestration potential of integrated regenerative farming and native vegetation restoration at farm scale.

projects.name: Renencom Afforestation/Reforestation Grouped Project
projects.description: Very small grouped afforestation and reforestation project in South Africa aggregating multiple tiny community tree planting activities. The extremely low credit volume of 66 tCO2e/yr indicates a demonstration-scale project.

projects.name: Soil carbon improvement Project through Grassland Management, in South Africa
projects.description: Grassland management soil carbon project in South Africa working with commercial livestock farmers to improve soil organic carbon through better grazing management. Developed by WeAct, the project applies the VM0026 methodology to measure soil carbon improvements from changed grazing practices on commercial cattle farms.

projects.name: Spekboom Restoration Project Beka Emva Igwanishe - Isixhosa
projects.developer: Imperative South Africa Projects Pty Ltd
projects.description: Thicket restoration project in the Eastern Cape restoring spekboom (Portulacaria afra) in degraded Valley Bushveld, a carbon-dense ecosystem unique to the Eastern Cape of South Africa. Developed by Imperative South Africa, the project name Beka Emva Igwanishe means put the tortoise back in isiXhosa, reflecting the restoration of habitat for the endemic and endangered Karoo padloper tortoise.

projects.name: The Soutpansberg Restoration And Conservation
projects.description: Forest and bushveld restoration project in the Soutpansberg Mountains of Limpopo Province, a biodiversity hotspot containing the highest diversity of tree species per unit area in South Africa. Developed by Few and Far, the project protects and restores the unique Soutpansberg afromontane forests, which form a critical water catchment for the Limpopo River system.

projects.name: Tree Planting in South African townships
projects.description: Community tree planting project establishing trees in South African townships through the Food and Trees for Africa programme. The project plants food-producing and shade trees in urban and peri-urban townships across multiple South African cities, improving urban greening and community food security at very small scale.

projects.name: uMkhanyakude Restoration Project
projects.description: Grassland and bushveld restoration project in the uMkhanyakude District of northern KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa, restoring degraded communal and private land through improved land management and active vegetation restoration. Developed by Afriwild Carbon, the project works in a region adjacent to the iSimangaliso Wetland Park UNESCO World Heritage Site.

projects.name: HIMA (Hifadhi ya Misitu ya Asili ya jamii) REDD+ Program
projects.description: Community forest management REDD+ programme in Tanzania protecting natural forest under community forest management agreements. Hifadhi ya Misitu ya Asili ya Jamii means community natural forest conservation in Swahili. The programme works with villages across Tanzania's coastal and inland forest zones to establish formal community forest management areas.

projects.name: Igombe REDD+ Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting miombo woodland and forest in the Igombe landscape of central Tanzania from clearance for charcoal production and subsistence farming. Developed by EcoNetix, the project works with villages in the Tabora and Katavi regions to establish community forest governance and conservation agreements.

projects.name: Longido and Monduli Rangelands Carbon Project
projects.description: Rangeland carbon project in the Longido and Monduli districts of Arusha Region, Tanzania, working with Maasai pastoralist communities to restore degraded rangelands through improved herd management. Developed by Soils for the Future, the project applies the VM0032 methodology to measure soil carbon improvements from rotational grazing on the semi-arid Maasai rangelands south of Mount Kilimanjaro.

projects.name: Makame Savannah REDD
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting the Makame Wildlife Management Area in the Manyara Region of northern Tanzania, a community-managed conservation area that provides a critical wildlife corridor between the Tarangire and Ruaha ecosystems. Developed by Carbon Tanzania, the project generates carbon revenues that fund community wildlife rangers and village conservation activities.

projects.name: Ntakata Mountains REDD
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting the Ntakata Mountains forest reserve in western Tanzania's Katavi Region, an area of exceptional miombo woodland and montane forest that connects the Mahale Mountains and Katavi National Parks. Developed by Carbon Tanzania, the project works with surrounding villages to protect one of East Africa's most remote and biodiverse forest landscapes.

projects.name: One Acre Fund Tanzania Agroforestry Program
projects.description: Agroforestry programme in Tanzania working through One Acre Fund's established farmer network to distribute and plant trees on smallholder farms. The programme integrates tree planting into One Acre Fund's agricultural support model, working with farmers in northern Tanzania to establish fruit and timber trees on farm boundaries.

projects.name: Reforestation of degraded grasslands in Uchindile & Mapanda, Tanzania
projects.description: Commercial reforestation project in the Uchindile-Mapanda area of the Southern Highlands of Tanzania establishing commercial pine and eucalyptus plantations on degraded grassland. Managed by Green Resources Tanzania, the project is part of a larger plantation forestry programme that provides both commercial timber and carbon sequestration.

projects.name: Ruvuma Wilderness Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting miombo woodland in the Ruvuma region of southern Tanzania, a remote and biodiverse landscape that forms part of a critical wildlife corridor connecting the Selous-Niassa miombo ecosystem. Developed by Carbon Tanzania, the project works with villages in Ruvuma Region to protect woodland from charcoal production and agricultural encroachment.

projects.name: Suledo Community ARR Project (Mradi wa jamii wa Suledo wa Hewa kaboni)
projects.description: Community reforestation project in the Suledo Community Forest Reserve in Kilosa District, Morogoro Region, Tanzania. The Suledo forest is managed by the Suledo community under a formal community forest management agreement, and this project funds active tree planting within the reserve to restore degraded areas.

projects.name: TIST Program in Tanzania, VCS-CCB-011
projects.description: TIST smallholder agroforestry programme component in Tanzania, applying the TIST model of GPS-verified smallholder grove planting to farming communities across the Kilimanjaro, Arusha, and Manyara regions. The CCB certification recognises the programme's community benefit-sharing and biodiversity outcomes from diverse native species planting.

projects.name: TanzaBlue
projects.description: Blue carbon project protecting and restoring mangrove ecosystems along the Tanzanian coast, one of East Africa's most mangrove-rich coastlines. Developed by Blue Forest, TanzaBlue works with coastal fishing communities to halt mangrove clearance for charcoal and pole timber, with carbon revenues supporting community conservation.

projects.name: Tausi Tanzania
projects.developer: New Forests Company Holdings I Limited
projects.description: Commercial reforestation project in Tanzania establishing native and commercial timber species on degraded land through the New Forests Company. The project is part of New Forests Company's broader East African plantation programme that combines sustainable commercial forestry with carbon sequestration and community employment.

projects.name: The Rubeho Mountains Carbon Project
projects.description: Reforestation project in the Rubeho Mountains of Dodoma Region, Tanzania, restoring native forest species on degraded hillside land in one of the Eastern Arc Mountains, a biodiversity hotspot containing hundreds of endemic species. Developed by Compassionate Carbon, the project works with highland farming communities to restore montane forest cover.

projects.name: Udzungwa Corridor Reforestation
projects.description: Reforestation project creating a forested corridor connecting fragmented forest patches in the Udzungwa Mountains of Tanzania, one of the world's most important centres of biodiversity and home to over 400 endemic species. Managed by Udzungwa Corridor Limited, the project plants native species to reconnect isolated forest fragments and enable wildlife movement.

projects.name: Village Climate Solutions
projects.description: Community-based REDD+ project working with villages in the Kilosa and Mvomero districts of Tanzania to protect natural forest and miombo woodland under community forest management agreements. Developed by Village Climate Solutions, the project works with communities to formalise forest governance and halt charcoal-driven deforestation.

projects.name: Avé Ga Grouped Project
projects.description: Grouped reforestation project in Togo aggregating multiple village-level tree planting activities into a single VCS project, covering the Avetonou and Gaga forest reserve areas. Developed by Reforest'Action, a French reforestation company, the project works with farming communities to plant native species on degraded land in the forest-savanna transition zone of central Togo.

projects.name: PlanTogo
projects.description: Very large-scale reforestation project in Togo establishing native and plantation tree species across degraded land. Developed by Sequoia Plantation Togo, the project aims to restore significant areas of degraded forest and savanna land in a country that has lost over 80% of its original forest cover.

projects.name: Togo Green Renaissance With Farmers and Schools
projects.developer: Reboisement et exploitation des forets SARL
projects.description: Community reforestation project in Togo working with smallholder farmers and schools to plant native tree species on degraded farmland and school grounds. The project uses school-based environmental education as an entry point for community engagement, with schoolchildren planting trees on school plots and farmers establishing woodlots and agroforestry systems.

projects.name: 1MTN Uganda Bamboo Planting Project
projects.description: Bamboo planting project in Uganda targeting degraded land on hillsides and stream banks with fast-growing bamboo species. Developed by 1MTN Nation, the project plants bamboo to restore degraded watershed land and provide communities with a sustainable source of construction materials and bioenergy, reducing pressure on natural forest.

projects.name: Agroforestry and reforestation with small-scale farmers in Uganda
projects.description: Small-scale agroforestry and reforestation project working with smallholder farmers in Uganda to integrate trees into farming systems. Developed by the PURE PROJECT, the project distributes tree seedlings and provides training to farming families to establish trees on their land, combining carbon sequestration with improved farm productivity.

projects.name: Bukaleba Forest Project
projects.developer: Busoga Forestry Co. Ltd (Subsidiary Green Resources)
projects.description: Commercial forest plantation project in the Bukaleba Forest Reserve in eastern Uganda establishing eucalyptus and pine plantations on degraded government forest reserve land. Managed by Busoga Forestry Company, a subsidiary of Green Resources, the project is part of the company's broader East African plantation programme.

projects.name: Kijani Forestry smallholder farmer forestry project
projects.description: Smallholder farmer forestry project in Uganda working with rural farming families to establish trees on their land through the Kijani Forestry model. The project aggregates thousands of small individual tree plantings on smallholder farms into a single grouped carbon project, paying farmers for each verified tree.

projects.name: Lake Victoria Watershed Agroforestry Carbon Project II
projects.description: Second phase of the Lake Victoria Watershed Agroforestry Carbon Project, expanding tree planting activities to farming communities in Uganda on the northern and western shores of Lake Victoria. Managed by Trees for the Future, Phase II targets the densely farmed and heavily deforested Lake Victoria watershed in Uganda.

projects.name: Mount Kei Reforestation Project
projects.description: Reforestation project on the degraded slopes of Mount Kei in northern Uganda establishing native forest species to restore watershed function. Managed by Equatoria Forestry Company, the project plants native trees on deforested hillsides to restore the water catchment of a mountain range that was heavily deforested for charcoal and agriculture.

projects.name: Natural Forest Regeneration For Enhanced Carbon Stocks In The Albertine Rift (Frecar)
projects.developer: World Wide Fund for Nature Denmark / Verdensnaturfonden
projects.description: Forest regeneration project in the Albertine Rift of western Uganda, one of the most biodiverse regions in Africa, supporting natural forest recovery on degraded land adjacent to protected areas. Managed by WWF Denmark in partnership with local communities, the project enhances connectivity between fragmented forest patches in a landscape critical for mountain gorillas, chimpanzees, and over 50% of Africa's bird species.

projects.name: Natural High Forest Rehabilitation Project on degraded land of Kibale National Park
projects.description: Forest rehabilitation project restoring degraded land on the boundary of Kibale National Park in western Uganda, one of East Africa's most important chimpanzee habitats. Managed by PRIMAKLIMA, the project plants native forest species on former agricultural encroachments within the national park boundary, expanding habitat for chimpanzees and red colobus monkeys.

projects.name: OYU - Reforesting Uganda for a better tomorrow
projects.description: Community reforestation project in Uganda planting native and commercial tree species on degraded land through OYU Green's established network of community groups and smallholder farmers. The project works across multiple districts to restore tree cover on degraded farmland and public land in a country that has lost over 60% of its forest cover since independence.

projects.name: OYU - Tenera Plantations by BIDCO
projects.description: Reforestation project linked to Bidco Uganda's palm oil operations, establishing native tree species and mixed forest on degraded land as a carbon and biodiversity offset for the company's agricultural footprint. Developed by OYU Green for Bidco, one of East Africa's largest edible oil companies.

projects.name: Sustainable Agricultural Improvement Practices in Uganda
projects.description: Agricultural land management project in Uganda working with smallholder farmers to improve soil carbon through sustainable farming practices. The project applies improved techniques including mulching, composting, and reduced tillage to build soil organic carbon on smallholder plots.

projects.name: Sustainable Wood by NFB
projects.description: Sustainable forestry project in Uganda establishing commercial timber plantations on degraded land as a sustainable alternative to natural forest logging. The project provides locally grown timber for construction markets, reducing pressure on natural forests while sequestering carbon in fast-growing plantation trees.

projects.name: TIST Program in Uganda, VCS 001
projects.description: First VCS-registered component of the TIST smallholder agroforestry programme in Uganda, establishing the model that has since grown to cover thousands of Ugandan smallholder farmers. The Uganda programme applies the same GPS-verified grove planting model as Kenya, working with farming communities in the central and eastern regions.

projects.name: TIST Program in Uganda, VCS 002
projects.description: Second component of the TIST Uganda programme, expanding tree planting to additional farming communities in central and western Uganda. The programme works with farmer groups organised into small clusters, each managing a portfolio of individual farm tree groves.

projects.name: TIST Program in Uganda, VCS 003
projects.description: Third component of the TIST Uganda smallholder agroforestry programme, adding coverage in the western and northern regions of Uganda. Uganda is TIST's second-largest programme country after Kenya, with tens of thousands of participating farmers.

projects.name: TIST Program in Uganda, VCS 004
projects.description: Fourth component of the TIST Uganda programme. The TIST model in Uganda works primarily with smallholder farmers in the districts of Kapchorwa, Mbale, Tororo, Manafwa, and Bududa in the slopes of Mount Elgon, as well as farming communities in central Uganda.

projects.name: TIST Program in Uganda, VCS 005
projects.description: Fifth component of the Uganda TIST programme expanding tree planting across additional smallholder farming communities. The programme's success in Uganda is attributed to the direct per-tree payment model that provides smallholder farmers with transparent and predictable income from their trees.

projects.name: TIST Program in Uganda, VCS 006
projects.description: Sixth component of the TIST Uganda smallholder forestry programme. Each TIST component represents a distinct period of tree planting registered under VCS, with older components now holding significant carbon stocks in mature trees that are remeasured and re-verified regularly.

projects.name: TIST Program in Uganda, VCS-CCB 010
projects.description: TIST Uganda programme component registered with CCB certification, recognising the community development and biodiversity benefits of the programme alongside its carbon credentials. CCB Gold certification reflects strong community benefit-sharing and diverse native species planting.

projects.name: TIST Program in Uganda, VCS-CCB 011
projects.description: Large TIST Uganda programme component with CCB certification, covering a significant portion of the programme's Ugandan grove portfolio. This component spans multiple districts and includes both tree species for commercial timber and native species for biodiversity and watershed function.

projects.name: The NFC Uganda Carbon Landscapes Project
projects.developer: New Forests Company Holdings I Limited
projects.description: Commercial forest plantation carbon project in Uganda established by New Forests Company on degraded land, growing native and commercial timber species across multiple planting sites. New Forests Company is one of East Africa's largest plantation forestry companies, with operations in Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, and Mozambique.

projects.name: Uganda Native Reforestation and Agroforestry Project
projects.description: Small-scale native reforestation and agroforestry project in Uganda planting indigenous tree species on degraded land through a community-based model. The very low credit volume indicates a pilot or demonstration-scale project.

projects.name: COMACO Landscape Management Project
projects.description: REDD+ landscape management project in the Luangwa Valley of Zambia developed by Community Markets for Conservation (COMACO), a social enterprise that pays farmers in food security commodities in exchange for conservation commitments. COMACO works with over 150,000 farming families across the Luangwa Valley to halt deforestation and improve agricultural productivity.

projects.name: COMACO Landscape Management Project - West
projects.description: Western extension of the COMACO landscape management programme in Zambia, expanding the community conservation model to the western Luangwa Valley and adjacent areas. COMACO's integrated approach links forest conservation payments with market access for sustainably produced crops and honey.

projects.name: COMACO Landscape Management Project-South
projects.description: Southern component of the COMACO landscape management programme in Zambia, extending conservation agreements with farming communities into the southern Luangwa Valley and surrounding game management areas. The project protects miombo woodland adjacent to South Luangwa National Park.

projects.name: COMACO South Landscape Management Project
projects.description: Agricultural land management component of the COMACO programme in southern Zambia, applying improved farming practices to build soil carbon on community farmland in the Luangwa Valley. This component complements the REDD+ protection components by improving agricultural productivity and reducing the need for further agricultural expansion into forest.

projects.name: Integrated REDD+ Project in Zambia
projects.description: Large-scale REDD+ project protecting miombo woodland across multiple forest concessions and community forest management areas in Zambia. The project works across a landscape of communal lands and forest reserves to halt charcoal-driven deforestation in one of Africa's largest remaining miombo woodland countries.

projects.name: Itabu Redd+ Project In Zambia
projects.description: Very large REDD+ project protecting miombo woodland and dry forest across a vast landscape in Zambia. At 9.3M tCO2e/yr of projected credits, this is one of the largest REDD+ projects ever registered, covering a massive area of communal and state forest land threatened by charcoal production and agricultural expansion.

projects.name: Kafue Zambezi Community Forest Project
projects.description: Community forest management project protecting miombo woodland in the Kafue-Zambezi landscape of western Zambia, a critical wildlife corridor between Kafue National Park and the Zambezi floodplains. The project works with communities in the Western Province to establish formal community forest management areas.

projects.name: Lavushimanda Community Conservation Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting miombo woodland in the Lavushimanda National Park buffer zone in Muchinga Province, Zambia. Managed by WeForest Zambia, the project works with communities surrounding the national park to reduce charcoal production and agricultural encroachment through community conservation agreements and alternative livelihood development.

projects.name: Lower Zambezi Redd+ Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting miombo woodland in the Rufunsa and Chirundu districts of Zambia's Lusaka and Southern provinces, adjacent to the Lower Zambezi National Park. Developed by BioCarbon Partners, the project works with communities in the wildlife management areas surrounding the park to halt charcoal-driven deforestation.

projects.name: Luangwa Community Forests Project
projects.description: Very large community REDD+ project protecting miombo woodland across community forest management areas in the Luangwa Valley of eastern Zambia. The project works with dozens of villages across the vast Luangwa Valley landscape to establish community forest governance and generate income from carbon credits as an alternative to charcoal production.

projects.name: Lumwana REDD+ Project
projects.description: Corporate REDD+ project developed by Lumwana Mining Company to protect miombo woodland adjacent to the Lumwana copper mine in northwestern Zambia. The project offsets the environmental footprint of the mine by protecting equivalent areas of natural woodland from charcoal-driven deforestation.

projects.name: Lunga Luswishi Carbon Enhancement Project
projects.description: Carbon enhancement project in the Lunga-Luswishi Game Management Area of Northwestern Province, Zambia, improving carbon stocks in the wildlife management area through better land management and reduced charcoal production. The project works with communities in the game management area adjacent to Kafue National Park.

projects.name: Mafisa Livestock Grazing Carbon Project
projects.description: Livestock grazing management carbon project in Zambia working with communal farmers to improve rangeland condition and soil carbon through rotational grazing management. Developed by ZayoHub Zambia, the project applies the VM0032 methodology to measure soil carbon improvements from changed grazing practices on communal pastoral land.

projects.name: Musokotwane REDD+ Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting miombo woodland in the Musokotwane Forest Reserve and surrounding communal lands in southern Zambia. Developed by Zambruss Enterprises, the project works with communities in the Livingstone and Kazungula districts to protect woodland from charcoal production and agricultural expansion.

projects.name: The Ecopreneur Movement – Miombo Woodland Restoration Project
projects.description: Large-scale miombo woodland restoration project in Zambia working with rural entrepreneurs to plant native miombo species and restore degraded woodland at landscape scale. Developed by Community Climate Solutions, the EcoPreneur model pays community members to grow and plant native trees, combining livelihood income with ecosystem restoration.

projects.name: Tombwe Smallholder Reforestation Project
projects.description: Smallholder reforestation project in Zambia working with small-scale farmers to establish trees on their land in the Tombwe area. Managed by Tombwe Processing, the project integrates commercial tree growing with carbon finance to provide smallholder farmers with an alternative income source from their land.

projects.name: Zambia Agroforestry Grouped Project
projects.description: Grouped agroforestry project in Zambia aggregating multiple community tree planting activities into a single VCS project through ETG Commodities. The project works with smallholder farming communities to integrate trees into agricultural systems across multiple provinces, providing fuelwood, fodder, and soil improvement alongside carbon sequestration.

projects.name: Zambruss REDD+ Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting miombo woodland in the Zambruss forest concession area of southern Zambia. Developed alongside the Musokotwane REDD project by Zambruss Enterprises, the project works with communities to protect woodland from charcoal-driven deforestation in the game management areas of the Kafue region.

projects.name: Chirisa REDD+ Project
projects.description: REDD+ project protecting the Chirisa Safari Area and surrounding communal land in Midlands Province, Zimbabwe, from deforestation for charcoal production and agricultural encroachment. Developed by Carbon Green Investments, the project works with communities in the Sengwa wildlife corridor to protect miombo woodland and wildlife habitat.

projects.name: Kariba Redd+ Project
projects.developer: Carbon Green Investments (project owner) - South Pole (former carbon asset developer, terminated 2023)
projects.status_note: Withdrawn from the Verra registry in May 2024 by project owner Carbon Green Investments following a Verra investigation that found 57% of the 27M credits issued were excess and did not correspond to actual emission reductions. South Pole, the carbon asset developer, terminated its involvement in October 2023. The project represents the largest integrity failure in the voluntary carbon market to date and is retained here as a reference case.
projects.description: The Kariba REDD+ project in Zimbabwe was one of the largest and most prominent voluntary carbon projects in Africa, protecting over 785,000 ha of miombo woodland in the Zambezi Valley across four communal areas. Developed originally by South Pole in partnership with Carbon Green Investments, the project became the subject of significant controversy in 2023 after investigations alleged substantial over-crediting. South Pole terminated its involvement in 2023. The project continues under revised management and methodology review.
